title: DAY 1: Foundations – Understanding the Social Media Landscape & Goal Setting
objective: By the end of Day 1, you will understand the major social platforms, their unique strengths, and how to set SMART goals that guide your strategy.

detailedContent.title: The Social Media Ecosystem
detailedContent.content:
**Not All Platforms Are Created Equal**

Each social platform has its own culture, content format, and audience expectations. Trying to be everywhere at once is a recipe for burnout. Today you'll learn what makes each platform unique so you can choose where to focus.

**Major Platforms at a Glance:**

| Platform | Primary Content | Audience Vibe | Best For |
|----------|-----------------|---------------|----------|
| **Instagram** | Photos, Reels, Stories | Visual, aspirational | Lifestyle, fashion, travel |
| **TikTok** | Short‑form video | Entertaining, raw | Reaching Gen Z, viral trends |
| **LinkedIn** | Professional articles | Career‑focused | Networking, B2B marketing |
| **Facebook** | Mix of text, images | Community, local | Groups, local businesses |
| **X (Twitter)** | Short text, threads | Fast‑paced | News, customer service |
| **YouTube** | Long‑form video | Educational | Tutorials, vlogs |
| **Pinterest** | Visual inspiration | Planning, DIY | E‑commerce, creative niches |

**The 80/20 Rule of Platform Selection**
Focus 80% of your effort on the 1–2 platforms where your target audience hangs out.

detailedContent.title: Why Goals Matter Before Content
detailedContent.content:
Most beginners start by posting randomly. Instead, every post should serve a **goal**. Goals give your content direction and give you a way to measure success.

**SMART Goals Framework:**

- **S**pecific – Clearly defined. Not "get more followers" but "gain 100 new followers."
- **M**easurable – You can track progress. "Increase engagement rate from 2% to 3%."
- **A**chievable – Realistic given your resources.
- **R**elevant – Aligned with your overall purpose.
- **T**ime‑bound – Has a deadline.

**Examples of SMART Goals:**
- Increase Instagram followers by 15% in 30 days.
- Generate 20 inquiries from LinkedIn posts within 2 weeks.

video.title: Social Media Marketing for Beginners – COMPLETE Strategy
video.description: Brian Dean covers goal setting and platform selection for beginners.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Platform Audit (15 minutes)
steps: List all social media platforms you currently use (even personally)
Identify what kind of content you see most and what makes you stop scrolling
Analyze a brand/creator you admire and their focus platform

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Define Your “Why” (10 minutes)
steps: Answer why you want to learn social media management
Identify who you want to reach (be specific)

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Set Your First SMART Goal (10 minutes)
steps: Write one goal you want to achieve by the end of this 7‑day course using the SMART framework
Choose your single focus platform for this course

reflectionPrompt: What excites me most about social media management? What feels overwhelming right now?

title: DAY 2: Content Strategy & Audience Persona
objective: By the end of Day 2, you will be able to define your target audience in detail and create a content strategy that speaks directly to them.

detailedContent.title: Know Your Audience Like a Friend
detailedContent.content:
You cannot create content for “everyone.” The most successful social media managers have a crystal‑clear picture of their ideal follower.

**Building an Audience Persona**
Give your ideal follower a name, face, and story. Know their demographics, interests, challenges, and goals. Now every piece of content you create can answer: *Would this person find this valuable?*

detailedContent.title: The Content Mix – The 4 Pillars
detailedContent.content:
A healthy social media strategy includes a variety of content types:

1. **Educational** – Teach something valuable (Tips, how‑tos).
2. **Inspirational** – Motivate or uplift (Quotes, success stories).
3. **Entertaining** – Make them smile (Memes, relatable humor).
4. **Promotional** – Share offers (should be ~20% of content).

**The Content Bucket Method**
Create 3–5 content “buckets” that align with your brand (e.g., Workout tips, Client transformations, Motivation).

video.title: Social Media Strategy: How to Create a Content Strategy That Works
video.description: Later walks through audience persona and content pillars.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Create Your Audience Persona (20 minutes)
steps: Write a detailed profile of your ideal follower: name, age, interests, challenges, goals
Identify where they hang out online

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Define Your Content Pillars (15 minutes)
steps: Brainstorm 4–5 content buckets for your brand
List 3 example post ideas for each bucket

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Analyze a Competitor (10 minutes)
steps: Find 2–3 accounts in your niche and identify their content pillars
Note which types of posts get the most engagement

reflectionPrompt: How does knowing my audience persona change how I think about content?

title: DAY 3: Content Creation – Visuals, Copy, & Storytelling
objective: By the end of Day 3, you will be able to create scroll‑stopping visuals and write compelling captions that drive engagement.

detailedContent.title: The Art of the Scroll‑Stopper
detailedContent.content:
On social media, you have about 3 seconds to grab attention. Your visual is what stops the scroll.

**Design Principles for Social Media:**
- **High contrast:** Readable text.
- **Bold fonts:** Use 1–2 fonts max.
- **Whitespace:** Don't clutter.
- **Human faces:** Attract more attention.

**Video Content**
Short‑form video (Reels, TikTok) currently has the highest organic reach. You only need a smartphone and good light.

detailedContent.title: Captions – The Second Hook
detailedContent.content:
Captions should provide value and include a call‑to‑action (CTA). 

**The AIDA Formula for Captions:**
- **Attention:** Hook them immediately.
- **Interest:** Build curiosity.
- **Desire:** Make them want it.
- **Action:** Tell them what to do next.

**Hashtags:** Use a mix of broad, niche, and branded hashtags (3–5 is often better than 30).

video.title: How to Create Scroll‑Stopping Social Media Graphics in Canva
video.description: Learn to design engaging visuals using free tools.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Design a Visual Post (15 minutes)
steps: Use Canva to create a visual for one of your post ideas
Ensure clear, bold text and consistent brand colors

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Write a Caption (10 minutes)
steps: Using the AIDA formula, write a caption for your visual
Include a hook, value, and a clear CTA

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Create a Short Video (15 minutes)
steps: Record a 15–30 second video from one of your content buckets
Edit in CapCut/InShot: trim, add captions, and background music

reflectionPrompt: Which format (visual vs. video) do I enjoy creating more? How can I improve my captions?

title: DAY 4: Scheduling & Automation Tools
objective: By the end of Day 4, you will be able to use scheduling tools to plan and automate your content, saving time and maintaining consistency.

detailedContent.title: Work Smarter, Not Harder
detailedContent.content:
Posting inconsistently is the #1 reason social media accounts fail to grow. Scheduling ahead maintains a consistent presence and reduces stress.

**Batching Content – The Efficiency Hack**
Set aside 2 hours once a week to create ALL your content for the week.

**Workflow:** Brainstorm -> Create visuals -> Write captions -> Schedule.

detailedContent.title: Optimal Posting Times
detailedContent.content:
General guidelines (test for your audience):
- **Instagram:** Weekdays 9 AM–11 AM.
- **LinkedIn:** Tues–Thurs 8 AM–10 AM.
- **TikTok:** Mornings 6 AM–10 AM or evenings 7 PM–11 PM.

**The Content Calendar**
Visual plan including Date, Platform, Content Type, Topic/Caption, and Status.

video.title: How to Schedule Social Media Posts (Beginner's Guide)
video.description: Tutorial on using tools like Later or Buffer to automate your content.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Choose a Scheduling Tool (5 minutes)
steps: Pick a tool (Buffer, Later, Hootsuite, etc.) and sign up for a free plan

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Batch‑Create & Schedule 3 Posts (30 minutes)
steps: Brainstorm 3 post ideas from your pillars
Create visuals/captions and load them into your scheduler for optimal times

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Create a Content Calendar (10 minutes)
steps: Set up a simple spreadsheet or Trello board to plan your week's content

reflectionPrompt: How does it feel to have content scheduled ahead? What were the challenges of batching?

title: DAY 5: Engagement & Community Management
objective: By the end of Day 5, you will understand how to build genuine relationships with your audience and manage interactions effectively.

detailedContent.title: Social Media is a Two‑Way Street
detailedContent.content:
Algorithms prioritize content that sparks conversation. If you 'post and ghost', your reach will plumet.

**The 80/20 Rule of Engagement**
Spend 20% of your time creating content, 80% engaging with others. Replying to comments and DMs is crucial.

**Handling Trolls:** Don't feed the trolls. Focus on constructive feedback and customer inquiries.

detailedContent.title: Building a Community, Not Just Followers
detailedContent.content:
Techniques:
- Ask questions and use polls.
- Go Live to interact in real-time.
- Feature user‑generated content.
- Be helpful in DMs, not salesy.

video.title: Social Media Engagement Strategy: How to Build a Loyal Following
video.description: GaryVee shares strategies for building a truly engaged community.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Engagement Audit (10 minutes)
steps: Review your last 5 posts—how many comments did you reply to?
Observe how a brand you admire handles their community interactions

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Proactive Engagement (15 minutes)
steps: Find 5 accounts in your niche and leave thoughtful, non-spammy comments
Note how many of them reply back

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Crisis Response Prep (5 minutes)
steps: Draft a professional response template for a hypothetical negative customer comment

reflectionPrompt: What felt natural about engaging with others? How can I make engagement a daily habit?

title: DAY 6: Analytics & Optimization
objective: By the end of Day 6, you will be able to read social media analytics, identify what's working, and optimize your strategy based on data.

detailedContent.title: Let Data Guide Your Decisions
detailedContent.content:
Analytics tell you which content resonates, when your audience is online, and who they really are.

**Key Metrics:**
- **Awareness:** Reach, impressions.
- **Engagement:** Likes, comments, shares, saves.
- **Traffic/Conversions:** Clicks, sign‑ups.

**Engagement Rate Formula:** (Total engagements / Reach) × 100. Average is 1-3%, 6%+ is excellent.

detailedContent.title: The Optimization Loop
detailedContent.content:
1. Post content.
2. Measure performance.
3. Identify patterns (why did a post work?).
4. Adjust strategy.
5. Repeat.

Use A/B testing: test different visuals or captions for the same topic to see what wins.

video.title: Instagram Analytics: The ONLY 5 Metrics That Matter
video.description: Learn to parse platform insights to improve your reach and engagement.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Performance Review (15 minutes)
steps: Identify your top 3 posts this week based on reach and engagement
Look for patterns: why did these succeed?
Identify a low-performing post and list 3 fixes

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Optimization Planning (10 minutes)
steps: Design a simple A/B test for your next post (e.g., test a question hook vs. a statement)
Write a data-driven goal for next week based on your findings

reflectionPrompt: What surprised me most in my analytics? How can I become less attached to my 'favorite' content and focus on what works?

title: DAY 7: Final Project – Create & Execute a 1‑Week Mini Campaign
objective: By the end of Day 7, you will have planned, created, scheduled, and begun executing a complete 1‑week social media campaign.

detailedContent.title: Campaign Execution
detailedContent.content:
Your final project is a 1-week mini campaign. 

**Components:**
1. **SMART Goal:** Specific and time-bound.
2. **Target Audience:** From your persona.
3. **Content Calendar:** 5+ planned posts.
4. **Engagement Plan:** Daily interaction strategy.
5. **Tracking Plan:** How to measure success.

video.title: How to Plan a Social Media Campaign (Step‑by‑Step)
video.description: Complete walkthrough of launching a strategic campaign.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Campaign Plan (20 minutes)
steps: Fill out all campaign components in your planner
Set a SMART goal for the week (e.g., 50 new followers)

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Batch Creation (60 minutes)
steps: Create all visuals and write captions for your 5+ campaign posts
Verify everything in your scheduling tool

exercises.title: Exercise 3: Final Self-Evaluation (10 minutes)
steps: Evaluate your planning process on a scale of 1-10
Set your mindset for the execution week ahead

reflectionPrompt: What was the most valuable lesson I learned this week? What is my next step?
